stories.title: SpaceX, OpenAI Windfall Fuels Bets on Next-Wave Asian AI Winners
stories.summary: The hunt is on for companies that could benefit from the tailwinds of an unprecedented wave of stock offerings in the US, and investors are increasingly honing in on the Asian supply chain.

stories.title: US Jobs Report Set to Reveal Solid Growth, Steady Unemployment Rate
stories.summary: Jobs week is coming up in the US, with a whole slate of indicators on the state of the labor market culminating on Friday with the government’s official report on employment for the month of May.

stories.title: Becerra Pulls Ahead in California Gubernatorial Primary Polls
stories.summary: Democrat Xavier Becerra has taken front-runner status in the California gubernatorial primary election on Tuesday, but the race is neck-and-neck for who his opponent might be in the general election.

stories.title: SoftBank Plans Up to €75 Billion Investment in French AI Centers
stories.summary: SoftBank Group Corp. plans to invest as much as €75 billion ($87 billion) to build 5 gigawatts of artificial intelligence data center capacity in France, saying the country is poised to become a top European hub for AI infrastructure.

stories.title: Can AI Grow Without Hurting Local Communities?
stories.summary: The rush to build AI data centers is drawing trillions of dollars in investment and long-term bets from infrastructure firms such as DigitalBridge. But in communities where those facilities are being built, residents and officials are raising concerns about electricity costs, water use, noise, transparency and who bears the risk if demand falls short. stories.title: Jes Staley to appear before Congress over ties to Jeffrey Epstein
stories.summary: Former Barclays boss agrees to in-person interview with US lawmakers over his relationship with paedophile

stories.title: Ferrari wanted to take on Chinese EVs with the Luce - then the backlash started
stories.summary: The brand's first EV has been heavily criticised, with some saying it has abandoned Ferrari's roots.

stories.title: Mexico Mayor Arrested as Extortion Crackdown Continues
stories.summary: Mexican authorities detained the mayor of a historic city near the capital, the latest move designed to show the government is rooting out corrupt politicians from its ranks.

stories.title: Jill Biden’s memoir revives painful memories as Democrats try to move on
stories.summary: First Lady’s book raises awkward questions for a party trying to rebuild itself for midterm elections

stories.title: Vitol Awarded Namibia Fuel Supply Deal From July to September
stories.summary: Namibia said it arranged an emergency deal with Vitol Group for three months of fuel supply through September in an effort to mitigate price shocks for oil products caused by the Iran war.

stories.title: Texas GOP Primary Upends Senate Race
stories.summary: Attorney General Ken Paxton benefited from a late endorsement from President Donald Trump and enthusiasm from Republicans’ MAGA voters to a historic blowout victory in the Senate primary runoff over incumbent John Cornyn. Bloomberg News Texas Bureau Chief Julie Fine and The New York Times Texas Bureau Chief David Goodman joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss

stories.title: Hegseth Reaffirms US Commitment to Pacific Allies
stories.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth praised defense allies in Asia and hailed newly stable ties with China, while taking swipes at longstanding security partners in Europe. Bloomberg News Global Defense Editor Gerry Doyle joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the comments Hegseth made Saturday at an Asia security forum in Singapore, which are the latest sign of

stories.title: Oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz might not return to levels seen before the Iran war
stories.summary: Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has seriously challenged freedom of navigation in the sea lane, raising questions about the future.

stories.title: Why Britain’s Bond Market Is Sounding the Alarm
stories.summary: As political uncertainty grows in the United Kingdom, investors are increasingly focused on the country’s fiscal outlook and rising government borrowing costs. Bloomberg Opinion Columnist John Authers says concerns over spending, debt, and political instability are pushing gilt yields higher, reviving memories of past market crises that helped topple governments from Harold Wilson to Liz Truss. Wi

stories.title: Hegseth Hails China Relationship at Defense Summit
stories.summary: Jabs at European allies and talks of stable US-China ties — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth took to the stage at a security summit in Singapore this weekend. But two topics were notably missing from his address: Taiwan and the Iran war. Haslinda Amin with more. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Crypto treasuries turn to risky equity in drive to raise cash
stories.summary: Companies that stock up on crypto assets are following Michael Saylor’s Strategy into so-called digital credit

stories.title: US Says Deals With Iran for Safe Hormuz Transit Are Prohibited
stories.summary: The US affirmed that deals with Iran to safely sail through the Strait of Hormuz — even those which don’t involve paying a toll — aren’t allowed, as the conflict between the two nations grinds on.

stories.title: SoftBank pledges €75bn to build Europe’s biggest AI facility in France
stories.summary: Masayoshi Son places France at the centre of his global AI ambitions

stories.title: Hegseth Praises Asian Allies, Takes Swipe at NATO
stories.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth praised defense allies in Asia and hailed newly stable ties with China, while taking swipes at longstanding security partners in Europe. stories.title: Analysis: An end to the Iran war may be just the beginning of a new era of U.S. inequality
stories.summary: Stocks have boomed while consumers have paid the costs of high energy prices.

stories.title: Meta has struggled at selling anything other than ads. Will AI be different?
stories.summary: Meta is making a major push to expand its business beyond online advertising, but past efforts show that success is far from guaranteed.

stories.title: Hegseth Says US Developing a ‘True Friendship’ With Pakistan
stories.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said “true friendship” is growing between Washington and Pakistan as Islamabad helps to negotiate an end to the war in Iran.

stories.title: Hanwha Aerospace Eyes Germany, UK Arms Deals as Demand Surges
stories.summary: Hanwha Aerospace Co. is in talks with Germany, the UK and other European nations for new weapons deals, seeking to capitalize on a massive wave of global arms spending triggered by conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East.

stories.title: How Switzerland Is Adpating to Melting Glaciers
stories.summary: Climate change is leading to the melting of glaciers and the thawing of permafrost worldwide.

stories.title: Trump ends Iran meeting without announcing ‘final determination’ on deal
stories.summary: Trump's Truth Social statement left unclear which of his conditions are already part of a deal that negotiators are working on to pause the U.S.-Iran war.

stories.title: Americans Hurt in Kuwait as Trump Sends Mixed Signals on War
stories.summary: Several Americans were hurt in a missile attack on a Kuwaiti air base over the past day as negotiations to extend the ceasefire with Iran dragged on without conclusion.

stories.title: NATO Military Head Says No ‘Drama’ With US
stories.summary: Europe has responded to President Donald Trump’s demands to pay more for defense, a top NATO military official said Saturday, adding that the relationship with the Pentagon is stable and free of “drama.” Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, chair of NATO’s Military Committee, spoke with Haslinda Amin at the Shangri-La Dialogue, Asia’s premier defense summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: New Zealand’s Penk on Hormuz Uncertainty (Video) (Correct)
stories.summary: New Zealand’s Defense Minister Chris Penk speaks to Bloomberg TV about how the Iran war is shaping his country’s security outlook, and the importance of freedom of navigation through the seas. He speaks with Bloomberg’s Haslinda Amin on the sidelines of the Shangri-La Dialogue. (Corrects name of interviewee.) (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Russia Recalls Ambassador From Armenia as Tensions Continue
stories.summary: Russia recalled its ambassador to Armenia for consultations in Moscow amid growing tensions with the South Caucasus country over its shift toward Europe.

stories.title: 25 African Startups to Watch | Bloomberg Next Africa
stories.summary: On this edition of Next Africa, we bring you Bloomberg's 2026 African startups to watch list. From Egypt to Mauritius, these companies are racing to solve some of the continent's toughest challenges. Plus, we hear from Moniepoint co-founder and CTO, Felix Ike, on building one of Africa's leading fintech platforms. We also speak with venture capital investors on opportunities and challenges of inve

stories.title: MiniMax Eyes China Listing, Takes on AI Rivals Like DeepSeek
stories.summary: China’s MiniMax Group Inc. has begun preparations for a domestic listing, according to a regulatory filing, as the fast‑growing artificial intelligence startup challenges local rivals including DeepSeek.

stories.title: Bulgaria to be punished for deficit only months after joining euro
stories.summary: European Commission to place Sofia under ‘excessive deficit procedure’ for breaching budget rules

stories.title: US agrees ‘perpetual’ futures trading after offshore Hyperliquid’s huge growth
stories.summary: Oil-linked trading on unregulated and decentralised crypto exchange has skyrocketed during Iran war

stories.title: BOE's Bailey Says UK Banks Don't Have Access to Mythos
stories.summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey says that British banks are still locked out of using Anthropic PBC’s powerful new AI tool. Speaking with Bloomberg's Stephanie Flanders at a central banking conference in Iceland, Bailey adds that banks are using other models to test their cyberdefenses. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: BOE’s Bailey Says UK Banks Still Lack Access to Mythos
stories.summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said that British banks are still locked out of using Anthropic PBC’s powerful new AI tool, calling for a international approach to fighting cybersecurity threats.

stories.title: Analysis Finds Trump's Deregulation Plan Falls Flat
stories.summary: A White House plan to rapidly repeal regulations has flopped, with just a handful of rules dumped via the expanded use of an administrative shortcut since a widespread review effort was ordered more than a year ago. Bloomberg Law Principal Legal Reporter Robert Iafolla joined David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Legacy Tech Stocks Surge on AI Pivot
stories.summary: Bloomberg Intelligence Global Head of Technology Research Mandeep Singh joined Christina Ruffini and David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss so-called "legacy" tech companies like Dell, Intel and Nokia seeing their stocks surge as they turn to AI. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Fed’s Williams Quips Economist Jobs Are Safe as AI Shift Unfolds
stories.summary: The implications of artificial intelligence gripped global central bankers in Iceland this week, with one Federal Reserve official joking that it won’t put economists out of business.

stories.title: Tech Stars From the 90s Reborn: Nokia, Dell, Cisco Surge on AI
stories.summary: They were all stars of the dot-com era before fading into the background as the bubble burst and a new generation of tech darlings rose to the forefront. But Dell Technologies Inc., Nokia Oyj and Lenovo Group Ltd. are back with a vengeance thanks to the unrelenting artificial intelligence spending boom.

stories.title: Philippines Seeks Closer Ties With Taiwan, Adversaries of China
stories.summary: The Philippines is seeking closer ties with Taiwan and stronger military links with nations focused on deterring China’s “nefarious plans,” according to the Southeast Asian nation’s defense chief.

stories.title: Berkshire trails red-hot S&P 500 by biggest margin so far this year
stories.summary: Berkshire's widely held B shares are now running 16.3 percentage points behind the benchmark index year-to-date, the biggest gap so far in 2026.

stories.title: Tokens or humans? The new corporate trade-off
stories.summary: AI is costing far more than companies expected, forcing CFOs into a new trade-off between tokens and humans and posing a risk the market hasn't priced in.

stories.title: AI Dangers Eclipse Nuclear Weapons at Singapore Defense Forum
stories.summary: The dangers of AI eclipsed those of nuclear weapons at a defense forum discussion on strategic stability in Singapore, as panelists warned it could reduce reaction times to the point where people make rash decisions.

stories.title: Hanwha Aerospace Eyes Europe, US Arms Deals
stories.summary: South Korea's Hanwha Aerospace is increasing weapons manufacturing at home, and expanding operations in Europe and the US. The company's Global Chief Strategy Officer, Alex Wong spoke with Bloomberg’s Haslinda Amin on the sidelines of the Shangri-La Dialogue, Asia’s premier defense summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: The Philippines is in for a 'long-term struggle' with China over territory, Manila's defense secretary tells CNBC
stories.summary: The Philippines is going to be locked in a long-term struggle with China over the South China Sea, the country's national defense secretary tells CNBC.

stories.title: Why does Castlelake want to buy easyJet?
stories.summary: Budget carrier has large fleet and expensive airport slots, while its share price has been hit by Iran crisis

stories.title: Anthropic Cuts Unauthorized Platform List by Half After Pushback
stories.summary: Anthropic PBC updated its warning about secondary markets for its shares, cutting the number of unauthorized platforms by half, after the notice caused panic among investors and a sharp rebuttal from one of the companies named.

stories.title: It is time for a European Security Council
stories.summary: Increasing US disengagement and continued Russian aggression have given the idea fresh impetus

stories.title: US, UK and Australia Deepen Subsea Security Pact With Drones
stories.summary: The Aukus alliance members — Australia, UK and the US — announced Saturday that they will develop advanced underwater drone systems and further deepen their maritime relationship that will see American submarines in Australia by next year.

stories.title: Remembering the Life and Work of Jonathan Clements: Masters in Business
stories.summary: Barry sits down with Jason Zweig and William Bernstein. They discuss "Money and Me" the last book of author and journalist Jonathan Clements. Jason and William also examine Clements's approach to personal finance and impact to financial journalism. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Malaysian Defense Minister Says Won’t Rush Spending
stories.summary: Malaysian defense chief Mohamed Khaled Nordin told Bloomberg that the country will not rush to increase its defence budget despite US pressure for partners to become more self-reliant. Khaled spoke with Haslinda Amin in an exclusive interview at Asia’s premier defense summit, the Shangri-La Dialogue. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Writing’s on the wall for the bond market – for those who can read it
stories.summary: There is a good deal more to the rapid rise in bond yields around the world, not least in Asia, than meets the eye. It suggests a recognition by financial markets that governments are spending beyond their means, tax revenues and borrowing power. The implication is that either taxes need to rise or public spending needs to fall, or alternatively that financial markets, stock markets in particular,

stories.title: How the Invention of Rope Gave Us Modern Civilization | Odd Lots
stories.summary: Rope is easy to take for granted. It seems obvious and straightforward. But of course, it had to be invented. Early humans discovered that by twisting fibers around each other, the resulting structure would be something durable and strong. Without rope, all kinds of things aren't possible, from lifting objects into the air to whaling or modern bridges. So how was rope developed and what were the b

stories.title: Trump will remain ‘patient’ in pursuit of a deal with Iran, says Hegseth
stories.summary: President wants to make a ‘great’ deal but defence secretary warns US is ready to restart attacks

stories.title: Europe Is Facing Up to a Painful Vulnerability to Drone Warfare
stories.summary: The crash of a Russian drone into a Romanian apartment building on Friday laid bare Europe’s lack of preparedness in confronting modern aerial warfare even as fighting rages across the border in Ukraine for a fifth year.

stories.title: Jim Cramer says Dell’s blowout quarter sets up a crucial week for AI stocks
stories.summary: CNBC’s Jim Cramer said Dell Technologies’ blockbuster quarter reignited enthusiasm around AI and data center stocks.

stories.title: SentinelOne stock drops 8% as cyber firm trims headcount to boost AI investments
stories.summary: SentinelOne reported earnings after the bell Thursday and issued lackluster guidance for the current quarter and full-year.

stories.title: More Fed policymakers eye possible rate hike as inflation risks rise
BOE's Bailey on AI Risks, Private Credit and Crypto
stories.summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey discusses the emerging risks from frontier AI models, the recent concerns about the private credit market and the central bank's stablecoin proposals. He speaks with Bloomberg's Stephanie Flanders at a central banking conference in Iceland. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Oil prices fall on hopes of Strait of Hormuz reopening
stories.summary: Biggest monthly decline in Brent crude since 2020 comes amid signs US and Iran could be close to a deal

stories.title: SpaceX Lowers IPO Valuation Target | Bloomberg Tech 5/29/2026
stories.summary: Bloomberg’s Tim Stenovec breaks down why SpaceX is coming back down to Earth with a slightly lower valuation in its IPO. Plus, Anthropic closes a funding round at a whopping $965 billion valuation, surpassing OpenAI for the first time in the AI race; and Dell surges after the hardware giant's outlook far surpassed Wall Street estimates. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Anthropic Valuation of $965 Billion Passes OpenAI
stories.summary: Anthropic raised $65 billion in a funding round that valued the AI company at $965 billion including the new investment, eclipsing rival OpenAI’s value for the first time. Bloomberg's AI reporter Shirin Ghaffary joins Tim Stenovec on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Fed Governor Michelle Bowman warns against hiking interest rates because of inflation spike
stories.summary: Bowman said reacting to the inflation surge, driven primarily by energy prices and tariffs, has proven ineffective.

stories.title: Nvidia is investing billions into this emerging technology that could change the AI industry
stories.summary: Photonics is considered to be a more efficient alternative to the current process of transferring data using electricity, which could be crucial to the AI boom.

stories.title: Dan Ives: Anthropic’s growth is 'just the tip of the spear' for AI rally
stories.summary: Ives' comments come as part of a wider prediction for the Nasdaq to top 30,000 points by 2027, reiterating his call from earlier interviews with CNBC.

stories.title: Samsung's shares surge as much as 6% after company ships next-generation AI memory chip samples
stories.summary: Shares of Samsung Electronics surged after the company began shipping HBM4E chip samples to its customers globally.

stories.title: The Tech Download: How chip companies are looking to use light to solve this major AI bottleneck
stories.summary: Nvidia is investing billions into companies developing photonics, which industry watchers say could bring big efficiency gains to the AI sector.
